commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a post-service-repair dashboard auth hint and focused tests for fresh dashboard token handoff plus token-safe repair messaging.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +141. Total +142 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. not for the full macOS upgrade/service-repair flow in this review. Source inspection and the linked issue logs support the missing-guidance path, but the PR body says no live service-repair E2E was run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is human/proof handling for a maintainer-labeled PR with mock-only real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(gateway): guide dashboard auth after service repair
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a post-repair hint that tells users to reopen the Control UI through `openclaw dashboard` or copy a fresh auth URL with `openclaw dashboard --no-open`.
- Add regression coverage that dashboard launch re-resolves config after gateway readiness recovery and uses the fresh tokenized URL.
- Add token-safety coverage for the service-repair message.

Fixes #88290.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/start-repair.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: After a gateway service repair, users with stale or missing Control UI auth state are directed to reopen through the dashboard command, which can hand off the current tokenized URL when safe.

Real environment tested: Local focused Vitest coverage in a Codex worktree; no live macOS upgrade/service-repair E2E was run.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/start-repair.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The dashboard test proves config is read again after readiness recovery and the fresh token URL is copied. The service-repair test proves the repair message contains only command guidance and does not expose the gateway token.

Observed result after fix: Focused tests passed, autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings, and `git diff --check` passed.

What was not tested: Full live macOS upgrade from `2026.5.18` to current with LaunchAgent repair and Safari localStorage state.

<!-- agent-transcript:start -->
## Agent Transcript

<details>
<summary>Redacted codex session transcript</summary>

````text
source: [LOCAL_SESSION]
redaction: local paths, emails, phone-shaped strings, token-shaped strings, auth headers, auth query params
omitted: raw tool outputs, system/developer prompts, local paths, secrets, browser/session/auth details
stats: {"agent":"codex","entries":17,"user":8,"assistant":8,"toolCalls":136,"toolOutputsDropped":135,"web":0,"redactions":2,"omittedUnsafe":0,"rawEntries":36}

[user]
please review this issue and suggest a fix - use a worktree since we're working on something else from our main checkout: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88290

[assistant]
I’ll use the OpenClaw PR maintainer workflow for the issue, and I’ll keep any investigation in a separate worktree so the current checkout stays untouched. First I’m checking repo state and the relevant maintainer instructions.

The main checkout is not dirty, but it is on a divergent feature branch, so I’m not going to push or pull that branch. I’m fetching `origin/main` and will base the issue investigation worktree on that instead.

`origin/main` advanced, so I’ll use the fresh tip for current behavior. I’m creating a dedicated worktree now, then I’ll pull the issue text, comments, assignment state, related cached threads, and the affected code path.

The issue is already assigned to `sallyom`, so I won’t mutate GitHub. The first worktree branch name hit a local ref layout conflict, so I’m retrying with a flat branch name and continuing with read-only issue/activity lookups.

The worktree is ready at `/private/tmp/openclaw-issue-88290` on `origin/main` commit `654de643e4`. I’m now checking the current auth/dashboard code and tests to separate what is already fixed on main from what still needs a policy/code change.

Current main already changed some of the bad client-side behavior: stale device tokens are cleared on `AUTH_DEVICE_TOKEN_MISMATCH`, and token-missing/device-token-mismatch are now non-retryable UI auth failures. I’m checking whether the remaining gap is the post-repair token handoff path or whether this is fully addressed after the reported `[REDACTED_PHONE]` build.

I have enough source context for the recommendation. I’m running the narrow existing tests that cover dashboard token delivery and the Control UI auth failure states, so the recommendation distinguishes proven current behavior from the remaining gap.

Used the OpenClaw PR maintainer workflow in a separate worktree branch `issue-88290-review-codex`.

By: Wault1408 (`@drarturryzhov-boop`, acct [REDACTED_PHONE]) | OpenClaw: 0 PRs, 10 issues, 0 commits/12mo | GitHub: 0 commits, 0 PRs, 10 issues, 0 reviews. Issue is already assigned to `@sallyom`.

My read: this is real UX friction, but current `origin/main` has already fixed the worst “silent loop” behavior. The UI now treats `AUTH_TOKEN_MISSING` and `AUTH_DEVICE_TOKEN_MISMATCH` as non-recoverable auth states (`ui/src/ui/gateway.ts:127`, `ui/src/ui/gateway.ts:132`), clears stale stored device tokens on mismatch (`ui/src/ui/gateway.ts:732`), and shows auth recovery guidance in the login gate (`ui/src/ui/views/login-gate.ts:204`, `ui/src/ui/views/login-gate.ts:217`).

Suggested fix: do not auto-auth bare dashboard loads and do not put SecretRef-managed tokens into URLs. Keep the guard in `src/commands/dashboard.ts:42`. Instead, make the service-repair path explicitly steer users back through `openclaw dashboard` after a repair, because `openclaw dashboard` already opens/copies a tokenized URL for non-SecretRef tokens (`src/commands/dashboard.ts:43`, `src/commands/dashboard.ts:95`) and re-resolves the dashboard target after recovery (`src/commands/dashboard.ts:76`). Add a focused test that `dashboardCommand` with `readiness.recovered: true` re-reads config and opens/copies the fresh token URL. Add a small service-repair test around `src/cli/daemon-cli/start-repair.ts:51` or the lifecycle caller to prove the repair output includes a non-secret hint like: “Gateway repaired. Run `openclaw dashboard` or `openclaw dashboard --no-open` for a fresh Control UI auth URL; do not reuse an old bare dashboard tab.”

The Codex harness symptom looks related to stale runtime during the upgrade window, not the same auth bug. `v2026.5.18` only allowed the Codex harness provider `codex`; current code allows `codex`, `openai-codex`, and `openai` (`extensions/codex/harness.ts:11`). If that recurs on a freshly repaired current gateway, route it through doctor/Codex route repair rather than coupling it to this Control UI auth fix.

Verified narrow current behavior with:
`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts ui/src/ui/gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/login-gate.test.ts`

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88290

[user]
let's prepare a fix in the worktree

[assistant]
I’ll make the fix in `/private/tmp/openclaw-issue-88290`, keeping the main checkout untouched. I’m using the OpenClaw maintainer workflow plus the testing guidance, then I’ll patch the narrow service-repair/dashboard coverage we discussed.

The change is narrow: one non-secret recovery hint in the repair result, plus tests that prove `openclaw dashboard` re-resolves a fresh tokenized URL after gateway recovery. I’m editing those paths now and leaving the auth-token SecretRef guard intact.

The first patch adds the repair hint and a regression test for fresh token handoff after readiness recovery. I’m running only the touched/adjacent Vitest files through the repo’s node wrapper, since this is a Codex worktree.

I adjusted the new service mock so it remains type-safe under TS checks. I’m rerunning the focused proof now.

The focused tests passed. I’m running the repo autoreview helper against the local patch now; if it finds a concrete issue, I’ll verify it against the code before changing anything.

Autoreview is still running without output; that is normal for this helper while the model call is active. I’ll let it finish and then either apply verified findings or report it clean.

Prepared the fix on branch `issue-88290-review-codex`.

Changed:
- `src/cli/daemon-cli/start-repair.ts`: after service repair, the CLI now tells users to reopen with `openclaw dashboard` or copy a fresh auth URL with `openclaw dashboard --no-open`, without exposing any token.
- `src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts`: added coverage that dashboard launch re-resolves config after readiness recovery and uses the fresh tokenized URL.
- `src/cli/daemon-cli/start-repair.test.ts`: added token-safe repair-message coverage.

Verified:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/start-repair.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `git diff --check`

Autoreview came back clean: no accepted/actionable findings.

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88290

[user]
what's the new message you added?

[assistant]
The new repair message is:

```text
Gateway service definition repaired and started. Reopen the Control UI with `openclaw dashboard` or copy a fresh auth URL with `openclaw dashboard --no-open`.
```

[user]
LGTM

[assistant]
Acknowledged. The fix is still uncommitted in `/private/tmp/openclaw-issue-88290` on branch `issue-88290-review-codex`, ready for commit/PR when you want it.

[user]
let's commit with --signoff and open a PR to fix 88290

[assistant]
I’ll commit only the three files in the issue worktree, with signoff, then push that branch and create a PR with the issue reference and verification. Before opening the PR I’m checking the repo PR/transcript instructions and the commit helper behavior.

The commit helper does not expose a signoff flag, so I’m using explicit `git add` plus `git commit -s` for this one, staging only the intended files. After that I’ll look for a safe local transcript candidate for the PR body, per repo workflow.

The signed-off commit succeeded as `6fb43c0ebb`. Git’s commit hook did run dependency setup in this worktree, so I’m checking for any incidental files before pushing.

A high-confidence local session transcript candidate exists for this PR. Include a redacted agent transcript in the PR body? It helps reviewers and can make the PR easier to prioritize; I can also open a local HTML preview first.

Include a redacted agent transcript in the PR body? It helps reviewers and can make the PR easier to prioritize. I can open a local HTML preview first if you want to inspect it.

[user]
can I see it first?

[assistant]
I’ll generate a sanitized local HTML preview from the matching session and open it for inspection. This stays local and does not touch the PR body.

Opened the local preview:

`/private/tmp/openclaw-issue-88290-agent-transcript-preview.html`

Review it and tell me whether to include the redacted transcript in the PR body.

[user]
did you run autoreview?

[assistant]
Yes. I ran:

```bash
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Result:

```text
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.86)
```

It noted the patch only expands the service-repair message and adds focused regression coverage for dashboard token re-resolution and token-safe repair messaging.

[user]
yes then please include and push a PR

[assistant]
I’ll include the transcript, then push the signed-off commit and open the PR against `origin/main`. I’m preparing the PR body first so the GitHub create call has the required summary, verification, real-behavior proof, and issue reference.

This checkout pushes through the `sallyom` fork remote, while `origin` push is intentionally disabled. I’m building the body and appending the transcript section, then I’ll push to `sallyom` and create the PR in `openclaw/openclaw`.

97 read, 3 write, 36 execute; raw tool outputs dropped: 135
````
